category_title: Year 5 pupils with MLD or BESD who exhibit high TA dependency and frequent off-task behavior
student_description: This student exhibits high dependency on Teaching Assistants (TAs) and takes a passive, often harmlessly off-task role (e.g., daydreaming or fiddling) in mainstream settings. They are frequently separated from the main classroom and peers, requiring a shift from TA-led task completion to teacher-led instruction, active cognitive engagement, and genuine classroom integration.

criteria.rationale: The student's high TA dependency stems from TAs taking on planning and teaching responsibilities, so the main teacher must reclaim this role to ensure high-quality instruction.
criteria.check: The long-term plan explicitly outlines how the main classroom teacher will take primary responsibility for the student's instruction, planning, and pedagogical support, reducing reliance on TA-led teaching.

criteria.rationale: The student experiences a high degree of separation from the class, teacher, and peers, which exacerbates their passive role and reliance on 1:1 adult support.
criteria.check: The long-term plan includes specific structural or organizational strategies to keep the student in the mainstream classroom and interacting with peers, explicitly avoiding routine withdrawal.
